[126:14]it's not really a big deal.

[126:17]A lot of people might say that by itself is not a problem.

[126:20]If you start to say, this is funny, or this is cool, or this is okay, who's to say that

[126:26]the next time it happens, somebody doesn't run up and punch him in the face.

[126:30]Now you got a really big problem.

[126:33]Who's to say that if somebody runs up and punches somebody in the face, the next person

[126:38]isn't going to go and maybe hit somebody with a baseball bat.

[126:43]Now that's a real problem.

[126:45]And that's not the type of society that I wanna live in.

[126:48]I don't wanna actually live in a society

[126:50]where people who are Jewish or happen to be Jewish

[126:54]are getting punched or beat up.

[126:57]And quite frankly, even heckled for that matter.

[127:00]People should generally speaking,

[127:02]and I say this as a content creator also,

[127:04]but also just in general,

[127:05]people should be able to go and live their private lives

[127:10]without being verbally or physically assaulted.

[127:14]And the moment that you start to say that you forfeit that privilege because of your politics,

[127:19]we're living an open warfare. And it cuts both ways. And I don't think that's any type of

[127:26]civilized way to live, that people should be getting screamed at or heckled or punched or beat

[127:33]up. I do actually believe in civility. And I think that's what America's about. I think that's Christian.

[127:40]And so I would caution everybody because don't get me wrong. I don't like Dave Portnoy. I think he's a poser. I think he's an asshole. I think he's a traitor.

[127:49]But if I saw him in the street, I wouldn't run up on him and yell, fuck you.

[127:54]Honestly, I wouldn't confront him at all. And if I did, I would be polite.

[127:59]I don't think that's the right thing to do. I don't think that's the right way to conduct ourselves. And I can't approve of it.

[128:05]And, I think that's bad in principle, but I'll tell you something else.

[128:09]And this is what I was maybe alluding to earlier when I say it goes further than this.

[128:15]Maybe you're unconvinced, maybe you're angry, maybe you're pissed off.

[128:18]You say, well, who cares?

[128:19]Somebody said F you, you know, they need to get the kind of hecklers veto.

[128:25]They need to get the mob veto over their behavior.

[128:28]If you're unconvinced, maybe you think this kind of thing is okay and I can't convince

[128:32]you otherwise, let me tell you where else this is going.

[128:36]You start to go and harass people in the street like this and it actually gives them the political

[128:45]enemy ammunition to go after us because you know what, we're right about what we're saying.

[128:55]If Jewish people like Larry Ellison are buying TikTok

[128:59]so that they can ban criticism of Israel,

[129:02]that's a huge problem.

[129:04]Like, we're right.

[129:05]There's a perfect example of organized Jewish influence

[129:09]that everybody can recognize as a conflict of interest.

[129:14]It is a verse or adverse, Petter said.

[129:18]It's adverse to a culture of free speech.

[129:22]And this is an instance of pro-Israel Jewish lobbying

[129:27]and regulatory institutional capture

[129:30]of private public institutions.

[129:33]It's a problem.

[129:34]And everybody would be on our side.

[129:37]Everybody, by the way, this is factual.

[129:39]It happened in reality.

[129:40]Everybody can get behind this, that that's a problem.

[129:45]But the moment that people start to go out there and say the message is F the Jews and

[129:51]We're gonna heckle and attack people in real life. Then you lose everybody

[129:56]Cuz nobody supports that

[129:59]The moment that you start to cross that line that very fine line and I don't think it's that fine

[130:06]But it's a hard line the moment that you cross that line you lose everybody

[130:13]You tell people we don't want a genocide in Gaza. They're with you. You tell people we don't want APEC in Congress

[130:18]They're with you. You tell people that we want to be able to criticize Israel on social media

[130:24]They're with you. We don't want Jewish billionaires buying TikTok in collusion with the government to censor

[130:30]Speech they're with you

[130:33]But the second that some punk, some asshole goes out there and starts yelling,

[130:38]hey, eff the Jews and throwing stuff at people, because they think it's funny,

[130:42]then you lose everybody.

[130:44]And everybody says, yeah, that's not cool, actually.

[130:49]That is actually a good example of so-called antisemitism.

[130:53]If you're attacking someone in public for being Jewish,

[130:56]you kind of lose that card.

[131:00]And that by that card, I mean, oh, so you're saying it's anti-Semitic to criticize Israel.

[131:06]That is ridiculous.

[131:08]But if you're trying to get people to defend, oh, you're saying it's anti-Semitic to say

[131:12]fuck the Jews and throw stuff at them in public.

[131:14]It's like, well, it, that kind of is, that is prejudice that, that is actually not cool.

[131:21]That's, that's immoral and everybody's going to say, yeah, like, no, we're not with you

[131:28]not only are you going to lose popular support, but you're going to taint the legitimacy of the

[131:35]rest of the arguments. And what's more, that just goes in the folder for the ADL. When the ADL

[131:42]goes to Congress and says there's been 50,000 anti-Semitic incidents this year, that's a real

[131:48]one that they can use, and they will use it. They will browbeat you forever, and they will use

[131:56]censorship. They will use that to bolster legislation. They will use that to bolster

[132:01]all kinds of things. So we need to be very clear. We are not about that. If you're doing

[132:09]that, you are not with us. So this kid, this 20 year old, do I think he should be charged?

[132:18]I don't know. I don't think that's really a crime. What he did. I generally consider

[132:27]and should he be thrown out of the university?

[132:30]At least 20 years old.

[132:31]I mean, 20 year olds do stupid stuff.

[132:35]I don't know that his future should be ruined for that,

[132:38]but you're not helping us.

[132:40]I don't know the name of the guy that did this,

[132:42]but whoever did this, you are not helping us.

[132:45]You are hurting us.

[132:46]And if you are hurting us,

[132:48]if you are an obstacle to our objectives,

[132:50]if you're making us look bad,

[132:51]we want nothing to do with you.

[132:54]It's one thing if you get canceled unfairly.

[132:57]It's one thing if you're helping us,

[132:59]we will circle the wagons, we will help you.

[133:03]But if you, because of your emotions,

[133:07]because of your impulsivity,

[133:09]because of your lack of prudence and temperance,

[133:12]if you go and do stupid stuff like this,

[133:15]you're helping the enemy,

[133:16]you're doing something indefensible,

[133:18]we cannot help you.

[133:20]We cannot defend that.

[133:22]And everybody needs to be absolutely crystal clear on this

[133:27]because mark my words, this kind of shit,

[133:29]it will be used against us.

[133:32]And like I said, I don't support it in principle.

[133:35]I don't support it by itself.

[133:37]I'm not just saying, oh, we can't do that

[133:39]because it'll make us look bad.

[133:40]No, this is asshole behavior and people should,

[133:45]I've never been an advocate for harassing people

[133:47]in public like that.

[133:51]That's not cool.

[133:52]Okay. And we do need to draw some boundaries on that. I saw Ian Carroll say something similar

[133:59]and I didn't watch the whole video, but a lot of people gave him a hard time. I think

[134:02]his heart's in the right place and he's totally correct. We do have to draw a hard boundary

[134:07]on that. I'm in favor of telling the truth. I'm in favor of infiltrating the institutions.

[134:19]I'm in favor of self-defense actually, but I'm not in favor of this kind of stuff where

[134:26]you go and make an asshole out of yourself in public, harassing people, kids, families,

[134:32]you know, people in public throwing stuff at them.

[134:34]That's not cool.

[134:35]And that's, it's a slippery slope.

[134:37]That's not a game you want to play, not a path you want to go down.

[134:41]You can't defend it.

[134:49]If Mark Levin was throwing stuff at white people in public, we would be up in arms too

[134:53]and we'd be justified.

[134:55]So you know, we have to call that out and say, that's not okay.

[134:58]You can disagree with Dave Portnoy, you know, and you could even go up and say, you know

[135:03]what, Dave Portnoy, I think you're a real asshole.

[135:06]I think you're wrong to be pro-abortion. I think you're wrong to be pro-Israel, but

[135:11]you can't be throwing stuff at him and he can't say fuck the Jews. You know, our message

[135:16]is not fuck anybody. Our message is we love America. We love Americans. Our message is

[135:22]positive, not negative. We need to be very clear about this.

[135:26]Because the second that you start to slum it with that kind of stuff, it really has a tendency to drag everybody down.

[135:33]And if you don't like this, good, leave.

[135:36]If you don't like that message, good, because you'll only be a problem in the future.

[137:00]interview.

[137:05]It doesn't take away from the fact that a lot of that is a deflection, but we do have

[137:10]to be self-policing.

[137:12]And a lot of people say, what's the boundary?

[137:14]The boundary is when you start saying, screw a whole group of people and we want harassment

[137:26]We want people with reasonable and justifiable positions

[137:35]to work at the Heritage Foundation,

[137:38]to be elected to Congress.

[140:20]because they recognize that Nick Fuentes has broken containment.

[140:32]and they objectively lost that battle.

[141:32]there's been this damage control effort.

[141:35]Shapiro has been leading the charge.

[141:37]And we talked about this, I think on Friday or Thursday,

[141:40]they're all saying something very specific,

[141:43]which I want to make clear.

[141:45]I want to reiterate this just briefly before we move on.

[141:50]They're all saying the exact same thing,

[141:53]and this is their playbook.

[141:55]It's not implicit.

[141:58]This is not a guess. This is not inside baseball.

[142:01]This is what they are broadcasting is their playbook.

[142:05]Like I said, Ross Duthat in the New York Times,

[142:08]he wrote this in an opinion piece, I think, on Friday.

[142:12]So you can go and read this.

[142:14]And Shapiro was saying this, and Levin said this at the Republican-Jewish Coalition.

[142:19]Their playbook is as follows.

[142:23]They acknowledge that they lost the battle

[142:26]to contain the ideas.

[142:28]You could say they lost the battle

[142:30]over the information space.

[142:33]They tried for 10 years and even longer to make it

[142:36]so that if you criticized Israel or talked about the lobby

[142:41]that you could not have a social media platform,

[142:44]you would literally be banned permanently

[142:47]on Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube.

[142:50]And if you were banned from social media, you could never reach a massive audience because

[142:56]that's where people get their ideas.

[142:59]So they tried that and for 10 years, they literally tried to suppress on the whole

[143:05]internet in all of America, anybody who would talk about these things and they failed.

[143:12]because now everybody's talking about these things.

[143:16]No one's being censored.

[143:18]And it seems increasingly that nobody is even being blacklisted.

[143:21]So they acknowledge, they say,

[143:23]okay, we tried to censor the whole internet and we lost.

[143:28]The internet is too powerful.

[143:30]And at the same time that they were making arguments

[143:33]about free speech,

[143:34]Which is a logical end of being a constitutional conservative. They were also trying to have it both ways and censor the things

[143:42]They didn't like colluding with the left. So maybe that was never going to last

[143:48]So they say we've lost that battle. We're in retreat

[144:04]Those that would criticize Israel.

[150:43]because you don't have power.

[152:10]which is sort of like an iron triangle
